heading: Premiums and bid amounts
label: 1395w–24 Premiums and bid amounts

L. 111–152, § 1102(a)Subsec. (a)(6)(B)(v). , which directed addition of cl. (v), was repealed by . As enacted, text read as follows:Pub. L. 111–148, § 3201(d)(2)(B)Pub. L. 111–152, § 1102(a)“(I) .—In order to establish fair MA competitive benchmarks under , the Secretary, acting through the Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (in this clause referred to as the ‘Chief Actuary’), shall establish—In generalsection 1395w–23(j)(1)(A)(i) of this title“(aa) actuarial guidelines for the submission of bid information under this paragraph; and“(bb) bidding rules that are appropriate to ensure accurate bids and fair competition among MA plans.“(II) .—The Secretary shall deny monthly bid amounts submitted under subparagraph (A) that do not meet the actuarial guidelines and rules established under subclause (I).Denial of bid amounts“(III) .—In the case where the Secretary determines that information submitted by an MA organization under subparagraph (A) contains consistent misrepresentations and failures to adequately meet requirements of the organization, the Secretary may refuse to accept any additional such bid amounts from the organization for the plan year and the Chief Actuary shall, if the Chief Actuary determines that the actuaries of the organization were complicit in those misrepresentations and failures, report those actuaries to the Actuarial Board for Counseling and Discipline.”Refusal to accept certain bids due to misrepresentations and failures to adequately meet requirementsSee Effective Date of 2010 Amendment note below.Subsec. (b)(1)(C)(i). , inserted “(or the applicable rebate percentage specified in clause (iii) in the case of plan years beginning on or after )” after “75 percent”.Pub. L. 111–152, § 1102(d)(1)January 1, 2012, which directed insertion of “(or 100 percent in the case of plan years beginning on or after )” after “75 percent”, was repealed by . See Effective Date of 2010 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 111–148, § 3201(c)Pub. L. 111–152, § 1102(a)January 1, 2014Subsec. (b)(1)(C)(ii). , substituted “rebate for plan years before 2012” for “rebate” in heading and “For plan years before 2012, a rebate” for “A rebate” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 111–148, § 3202(b)(1)(A)Subsec. (b)(1)(C)(iii). , added cl. (iii) and struck out former cl. (iii). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For plan years beginning on or after , a rebate required under this subparagraph may not be used for the purpose described in clause (ii)(III) and shall be provided through the application of the amount of the rebate in the following priority order:Pub. L. 111–152, § 1102(d)(2)January 1, 2012“(I) First, to use the most significant share to meaningfully reduce cost-sharing otherwise applicable for benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program under parts A and B and for qualified prescription drug coverage under part D, including the reduction of any deductibles, copayments, and maximum limitations on out-of-pocket expenses otherwise applicable. Any reduction of maximum limitations on out-of-pocket expenses under the preceding sentence shall apply to all benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option. The Secretary may provide guidance on meaningfully reducing cost-sharing under this subclause, except that such guidance may not require a particular amount of cost-sharing or reduction in cost-sharing.“(II) Second, to use the next most significant share to meaningfully provide coverage of preventive and wellness health care benefits (as defined by the Secretary) which are not benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program, such as smoking cessation, a free flu shot, and an annual physical examination.“(III) Third, to use the remaining share to meaningfully provide coverage of other health care benefits which are not benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program, such as eye examinations and dental coverage, and are not benefits described in subclause (II).”, added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 111–148, § 3202(b)(1)(C)Subsec. (b)(1)(C)(iv). , added cl. (iv). Former cl. (iv) redesignated (vii).Pub. L. 111–152, § 1102(d)(2), redesignated cl. (iii) as (iv).Pub. children.heading: Submission of proposed premiums, bid amounts, and related information
children.label: a Submission of proposed premiums, bid amounts, and related information
i
children.text: The information described in paragraph (2), (3), (4), or (6)(A) for the type of plan and year involved.

children.label: ii
children.text: The plan type for each plan.

children.label: iii
children.text: The enrollment capacity (if any) in relation to the plan and area.

children.label: i
children.text: the manner in which such rebate will be provided under clause (ii) of such subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium (if any) and the MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (if any).

children.heading: Paperwork reduction for offering of MA regional plans nationally or in multi-region areas
children.label: C Paperwork reduction for offering of MA regional plans nationally or in multi-region areas
children.text: The Secretary shall establish requirements for information submission under this subsection in a manner that promotes the offering of MA regional plans in more than one region (including all regions) through the filing of consolidated information.

children.heading: Information required for coordinated care plans before 2006
children.label: 2 Information required for coordinated care plans before 2006
i
children.text: the adjusted community rate (as defined in subsection (f)(3));

children.label: ii
children.text: the Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium (as defined in subsection (b)(2)(A));

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable under the plan and the actuarial value of such deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments, described in subsection (e)(1)(A); and

children.label: iv
children.text: if required under subsection (f)(1), a description of the additional benefits to be provided pursuant to such subsection and the value determined for such proposed benefits under such subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: the adjusted community rate (as defined in subsection (f)(3));

children.label: ii
children.text: the Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (as defined in subsection (b)(2)(B)); and

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable under the plan and the actuarial value of such deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments, described in subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Basic (and additional) benefits
children.label: A Basic (and additional) benefits
children.text: For benefits described in , the amount of the Medicare+Choice monthly MSA premium.section 1395w–22(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Supplemental benefits
children.label: B Supplemental benefits
children.text: For benefits described in , the amount of the Medicare+Choice monthly supplementary beneficiary premium.section 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Requirements for private fee-for-service plans before 2006
children.label: 4 Requirements for private fee-for-service plans before 2006
i
children.text: the adjusted community rate (as defined in subsection (f)(3));

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium;

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable under the plan, and the actuarial value of such deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments, as described in subsection (e)(4)(A); and

children.label: iv
children.text: if required under subsection (f)(1), a description of the additional benefits to be provided pursuant to such subsection and the value determined for such proposed benefits under such subsection.

children.heading: Supplemental benefits
children.label: B Supplemental benefits
children.text: For benefits described in , the amount of the Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (as defined in subsection (b)(2)(B)).section 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall review the adjusted community rates, the amounts of the basic and supplemental premiums, and values filed under paragraphs (2) and (4) of this subsection and shall approve or disapprove such rates, amounts, and values so submitted. The Chief Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services shall review the actuarial assumptions and data used by the Medicare+Choice organization with respect to such rates, amounts, and values so submitted to determine the appropriateness of such assumptions and data.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The Secretary shall not review, approve, or disapprove the amounts submitted under paragraph (3) or, in the case of an MA private fee-for-service plan, subparagraphs (A)(ii) and (B) of paragraph (4).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed as requiring the Secretary to accept any or every bid submitted by an MA organization under this subsection.

children.heading: Authority to deny bids that propose significant increases in cost sharing or decreases in benefits
children.label: ii Authority to deny bids that propose significant increases in cost sharing or decreases in benefits
children.text: The Secretary may deny a bid submitted by an MA organization for an MA plan if it proposes significant increases in cost sharing or decreases in benefits offered under the plan.

children.heading: Submission of bid amounts by MA organizations beginning in 2006
children.label: 6 Submission of bid amounts by MA organizations beginning in 2006
i
children.text: The monthly aggregate bid amount for the provision of all items and services under the plan, which amount shall be based on average revenue requirements (as used for purposes of ) in the payment area for an enrollee with a national average risk profile for the factors described in (as specified by the Secretary).section 300e–1(8) of this titlesection 1395w–23(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the provision of benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option (as defined in ), including, for plan year 2020 and subsequent plan years, the provision of additional telehealth benefits as described in ;section 1395w–22(a)(1)(B) of this titlesection 1395w–22(m) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the provision of basic prescription drug coverage; and

children.label: III
children.text: the provision of supplemental health care benefits.

children.label: iii
children.text: The actuarial basis for determining the amount under clause (i) and the proportions described in clause (ii) and such additional information as the Secretary may require to verify such actuarial bases and the projected number of enrollees in each MA local area.

children.label: iv
children.text: A description of deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable under the plan and the actuarial value of such deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments, described in subsection (e)(4)(A).

children.label: v
children.text: With respect to qualified prescription drug coverage, the information required under , as incorporated under , with respect to such coverage.section 1395w–104 of this titlesection 1395w–111(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Acceptance and negotiation of bid amounts
children.label: B Acceptance and negotiation of bid amounts

children.heading: Authority
children.label: i Authority
children.text: Subject to clauses (iii) and (iv), the Secretary has the authority to negotiate regarding monthly bid amounts submitted under subparagraph (A) (and the proportions described in subparagraph (A)(ii)), including supplemental benefits provided under subsection (b)(1)(C)(ii)(I) and in exercising such authority the Secretary shall have authority similar to the authority of the Director of the Office of Personnel Management with respect to health benefits plans under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.heading: Application of FEHBP standard
children.label: ii Application of FEHBP standard
children.text: Subject to clause (iv), the Secretary may only accept such a bid amount or proportion if the Secretary determines that such amount and proportions are supported by the actuarial bases provided under subparagraph (A) and reasonably and equitably reflects the revenue requirements (as used for purposes of ) of benefits provided under that plan.section 300e–1(8) of this title

children.heading: Noninterference
children.label: iii Noninterference
children.text: In order to promote competition under this part and part D and in carrying out such parts, the Secretary may not require any MA organization to contract with a particular hospital, physician, or other entity or individual to furnish items and services under this subchapter or require a particular price structure for payment under such a contract to the extent consistent with the Secretary’s authority under this part.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: iv Exception
children.text: In the case of a plan described in , the provisions of clauses (i) and (ii) shall not apply and the provisions of paragraph (5)(B), prohibiting the review, approval, or disapproval of amounts described in such paragraph, shall apply to the negotiation and rejection of the monthly bid amounts and the proportions referred to in subparagraph (A).section 1395w–21(a)(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Rule for other than MSA plans
children.label: A Rule for other than MSA plans
children.text: Subject to the rebate under subparagraph (C), the monthly amount (if any) of the premium charged to an individual enrolled in a Medicare+Choice plan (other than an MSA plan) offered by a Medicare+Choice organization shall be equal to the sum of the Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium, the Medicare+Choice monthly supplementary beneficiary premium (if any), and, if the plan provides qualified prescription drug coverage, the MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium.

children.heading: MSA plans
children.label: B MSA plans
children.text: The monthly amount of the premium charged to an individual enrolled in an MSA plan offered by a Medicare+Choice organization shall be equal to the Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (if any).

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: i Requirement
children.text: The MA plan shall provide to the enrollee a monthly rebate equal to 75 percent (or the applicable rebate percentage specified in clause (iii) in the case of plan years beginning on or after ) of the average per capita savings (if any) described in paragraph (3)(C) or (4)(C), as applicable to the plan and year involved.January 1, 2012

children.heading: Form of rebate for plan years before 2012
children.label: ii Form of rebate for plan years before 2012

children.heading: Provision of supplemental health care benefits and payment for premium for supplemental benefits
children.label: I Provision of supplemental health care benefits and payment for premium for supplemental benefits
children.text: The provision of supplemental health care benefits described in in a manner specified under the plan, which may include the reduction of cost-sharing otherwise applicable as well as additional health care benefits which are not benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option, or crediting toward an MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (if any).section 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Payment for premium for prescription drug coverage
children.label: II Payment for premium for prescription drug coverage
children.text: Crediting toward the MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium.

children.heading: Payment toward part B premium
children.label: III Payment toward part B premium
children.text: Crediting toward the premium imposed under part B (determined without regard to the application of subsections (b), (h), and (i) of ).section 1395r of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the product of the old phase-in proportion for the year under clause (iv) and 75 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: the product of the new phase-in proportion for the year under clause (iv) and the final applicable rebate percentage under clause (v).

children.heading: Old and new phase-in proportions
children.label: iv Old and new phase-in proportions
I
children.text: for 2012, the old phase-in proportion is ⅔ and the new phase-in proportion is ⅓;

children.label: II
children.text: for 2013, the old phase-in proportion is ⅓ and the new phase-in proportion is ⅔; and

children.label: III
children.text: for 2014 and any subsequent year, the old phase-in proportion is 0 and the new phase-in proportion is 1.

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a plan with a quality rating under such system of at least 4.5 stars, 70 percent;

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of a plan with a quality rating under such system of at least 3.5 stars and less than 4.5 stars, 65 percent; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a plan with a quality rating under such system of less than 3.5 stars, 50 percent.

children.heading: Treatment of low enrollment and new plans
children.label: vi Treatment of low enrollment and new plans
I
children.text: for 2012, in the case of a plan described in subclause (I) of subsection ()(3)(A)(ii), the plan shall be treated as having a rating of 4.5 stars; ando1So in original. Probably means subclause (I) of section 1395w–23()(3)(A)(ii) of this title.1o

children.label: II
children.text: for 2012 or a subsequent year, in the case of a new MA plan (as defined under subclause (III) of subsection ()(3)(A)(iii) ) that is treated as a qualifying plan pursuant to subclause (I) of such subsection, the plan shall be treated as having a rating of 3.5 stars.o2So in original. Probably means subclause (II) of section 1395w–23()(3)(A)(iii) of this title.2o

children.heading: Disclosure relating to rebates
children.label: vii Disclosure relating to rebates
children.text: The plan shall disclose to the Secretary information on the form and amount of the rebate provided under this subparagraph or the actuarial value in the case of supplemental health care benefits.

children.heading: Application of part B premium reduction
children.label: viii Application of part B premium reduction
children.text: Insofar as an MA organization elects to provide a rebate under this subparagraph under a plan as a credit toward the part B premium under clause (ii)(III), the Secretary shall apply such credit to reduce the premium under of each enrollee in such plan as provided in .section 1395r of this titlesection 1395s(i) of this title

children.heading: MA monthly basic beneficiary premium
children.label: A MA monthly basic beneficiary premium
i
children.text: described in (relating to plans providing rebates), zero; orsection 1395w–23(a)(1)(B)(i) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: described in , the amount (if any) by which the unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount (as defined in subparagraph (E)) exceeds the applicable unadjusted MA area-specific non-drug monthly benchmark amount (as defined in ).section 1395w–23(a)(1)(B)(ii) of this titlesection 1395w–23(j) of this title

children.heading: MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium
children.label: B MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium
children.text: The term “MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium” means, with respect to an MA plan, the base beneficiary premium (as determined under paragraph (2) or (8) (as applicable) of and as adjusted under ), less the amount of rebate credited toward such amount under subsection (b)(1)(C)(ii)(II).section 1395w–113(a) of this titlesection 1395w–113(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium
children.label: C MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium” means, with respect to an MA plan, the portion of the aggregate monthly bid amount submitted under clause (i) of subsection (a)(6)(A) for the year that is attributable under clause (ii)(III) of such subsection to the provision of supplemental health care benefits, less the amount of rebate credited toward such portion under subsection (b)(1)(C)(ii)(I).

children.heading: Application of MA monthly supplementary beneficiary premium
children.label: ii Application of MA monthly supplementary beneficiary premium
children.text: For plan years beginning on or after , any MA monthly supplementary beneficiary premium charged to an individual enrolled in an MA plan shall be used for the purposes, and in the priority order, described in subclauses (I) through (III) of paragraph (1)(C)(iii).January 1, 20123See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: Medicare+Choice monthly MSA premium
children.label: D Medicare+Choice monthly MSA premium
children.text: The term “Medicare+Choice monthly MSA premium” means, with respect to a Medicare+Choice plan, the amount of such premium filed under subsection (a)(3)(A) for the plan.

children.heading: Unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount
children.label: E Unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount
children.text: The term “unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount” means the portion of the bid amount submitted under clause (i) of subsection (a)(6)(A) for the year that is attributable under clause (ii)(I) of such subsection to the provision of benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option (as defined in ).section 1395w–22(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Computation of average per capita monthly savings for local plans
children.label: 3 Computation of average per capita monthly savings for local plans

children.heading: Determination of statewide average risk adjustment for local plans
children.label: A Determination of statewide average risk adjustment for local plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (iii), the Secretary shall determine, at the same time rates are promulgated under (beginning with 2006) for each State, the average of the risk adjustment factors to be applied under to payment for enrollees in that State for MA local plans.section 1395w–23(b)(1) of this titlesection 1395w–23(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of States for first year in which local plan offered
children.label: ii Treatment of States for first year in which local plan offered
children.text: In the case of a State in which no MA local plan was offered in the previous year, the Secretary shall estimate such average. In making such estimate, the Secretary may use average risk adjustment factors applied to comparable States or applied on a national basis.

children.heading: Authority to determine risk adjustment for areas other than States
children.label: iii Authority to determine risk adjustment for areas other than States
children.text: The Secretary may provide for the determination and application of risk adjustment factors under this subparagraph on the basis of areas other than States or on a plan-specific basis.

children.heading: Determination of risk adjusted benchmark and risk-adjusted bid for local plans
children.label: B Determination of risk adjusted benchmark and risk-adjusted bid for local plans
i
children.text: adjust the applicable MA area-specific non-drug monthly benchmark amount (as defined in ) for the area by the average risk adjustment factor computed under subparagraph (A); andsection 1395w–23(j)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: adjust the unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount by such applicable average risk adjustment factor.

children.heading: Determination of average per capita monthly savings
children.label: C Determination of average per capita monthly savings
i
children.text: the risk-adjusted benchmark amount computed under subparagraph (B)(i); exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: the risk-adjusted bid computed under subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Computation of average per capita monthly savings for regional plans
children.label: 4 Computation of average per capita monthly savings for regional plans

children.heading: Determination of regionwide average risk adjustment for regional plans
children.label: A Determination of regionwide average risk adjustment for regional plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall determine, at the same time rates are promulgated under (beginning with 2006) for each MA region the average of the risk adjustment factors to be applied under to payment for enrollees in that region for MA regional plans.section 1395w–23(b)(1) of this titlesection 1395w–23(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of regions for first year in which regional plan offered
children.label: ii Treatment of regions for first year in which regional plan offered
children.text: In the case of an MA region in which no MA regional plan was offered in the previous year, the Secretary shall estimate such average. In making such estimate, the Secretary may use average risk adjustment factors applied to comparable regions or applied on a national basis.

children.heading: Authority to determine risk adjustment for areas other than regions
children.label: iii Authority to determine risk adjustment for areas other than regions
children.text: The Secretary may provide for the determination and application of risk adjustment factors under this subparagraph on the basis of areas other than MA regions or on a plan-specific basis.

children.heading: Determination of risk-adjusted benchmark and risk-adjusted bid for regional plans
children.label: B Determination of risk-adjusted benchmark and risk-adjusted bid for regional plans
i
children.text: adjust the applicable MA area-specific non-drug monthly benchmark amount (as defined in ) for the region by the average risk adjustment factor computed under subparagraph (A); andsection 1395w–23(j)(2) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: adjust the unadjusted MA statutory non-drug monthly bid amount by such applicable average risk adjustment factor.

children.heading: Determination of average per capita monthly savings
children.label: C Determination of average per capita monthly savings
i
children.text: the risk-adjusted benchmark amount computed under subparagraph (B)(i); exceeds

children.label: ii
children.text: the risk-adjusted bid computed under subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Uniform premium and bid amounts
children.label: c Uniform premium and bid amounts
children.text: Except as permitted under , the MA monthly bid amount submitted under subsection (a)(6), the amounts of the MA monthly basic, prescription drug, and supplemental beneficiary premiums, and the MA monthly MSA premium charged under subsection (b) of an MA organization under this part may not vary among individuals enrolled in the plan.section 1395w–27(i) of this title

children.heading: Terms and conditions of imposing premiums
children.label: d Terms and conditions of imposing premiums

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each Medicare+Choice organization shall permit the payment of Medicare+Choice monthly basic, prescription drug, and supplemental beneficiary premiums on a monthly basis, may terminate election of individuals for a Medicare+Choice plan for failure to make premium payments only in accordance with , and may not provide for cash or other monetary rebates as an inducement for enrollment or otherwise.section 1395w–21(g)(3)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Beneficiary’s option of payment through withholding from social security payment or use of electronic funds transfer mechanism
children.label: 2 Beneficiary’s option of payment through withholding from social security payment or use of electronic funds transfer mechanism
A
children.text: withholding from benefit payments in the manner provided under with respect to monthly premiums under ;section 1395s of this titlesection 1395r of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an electronic funds transfer mechanism (such as automatic charges of an account at a financial institution or a credit or debit card account); or

children.label: C
children.text: such other means as the Secretary may specify, including payment by an employer or under employment-based retiree health coverage (as defined in ) on behalf of an employee or former employee (or dependent).section 1395w–132(c)(1) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: by the beginning of each year, the name, social security account number, consolidated monthly beneficiary premium described in paragraph (4) owed by such enrollee for each month during the year, and other information determined appropriate by the Secretary, in consultation with the Commissioner of Social Security; and

children.label: B
children.text: periodically throughout the year, information to update the information previously transmitted under this paragraph for the year.

children.label: A
children.text: the MA monthly basic beneficiary premium (if any);

children.label: B
children.text: the MA monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (if any); and

children.label: C
children.text: the MA monthly prescription drug beneficiary premium (if any).

children.heading: For basic and additional benefits before 2006
children.label: 1 For basic and additional benefits before 2006
A
children.text: the Medicare+Choice monthly basic beneficiary premium (multiplied by 12) and the actuarial value of the deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable on average to individuals enrolled under this part with a Medicare+Choice plan described in of an organization with respect to required benefits described in and additional benefits (if any) required under subsection (f)(1)(A) for a year, exceedsection 1395w–21(a)(2)(A) of this titlesection 1395w–22(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the actuarial value of the deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments that would be applicable on average to individuals entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled under part B if they were not members of a Medicare+Choice organization for the year.

children.heading: For supplemental benefits before 2006
children.label: 2 For supplemental benefits before 2006
children.text: For periods before 2006, if the Medicare+Choice organization provides to its members enrolled under this part in a Medicare+Choice plan described in with respect to supplemental benefits described in , the sum of the Medicare+Choice monthly supplemental beneficiary premium (multiplied by 12) charged and the actuarial value of its deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments charged with respect to such benefits may not exceed the adjusted community rate for such benefits (as defined in subsection (f)(3)).section 1395w–21(a)(2)(A) of this titlesection 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Determination on other basis
children.label: 3 Determination on other basis
children.text: If the Secretary determines that adequate data are not available to determine the actuarial value under paragraph (1)(A), (2), or (4), the Secretary may determine such amount with respect to all individuals in same geographic area, the State, or in the United States, eligible to enroll in the Medicare+Choice plan involved under this part or on the basis of other appropriate data.

children.heading: Special rule for private fee-for-service plans and for basic benefits beginning in 2006
children.label: 4 Special rule for private fee-for-service plans and for basic benefits beginning in 2006
A
children.text: the actuarial value of the deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments applicable on average to individuals enrolled under this part with such a plan of an organization with respect to benefits under the original medicare fee-for-service program option, exceed

children.label: B
children.text: the actuarial value of the deductibles, coinsurance, and copayments that would be applicable with respect to such benefits on average to individuals entitled to benefits under part A and enrolled under part B if they were not members of a Medicare+Choice organization for the year.

children.heading: Requirement for additional benefits before 2006
children.label: f Requirement for additional benefits before 2006

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For years before 2006, each Medicare+Choice organization (in relation to a Medicare+Choice plan, other than an MSA plan, it offers) shall provide that if there is an excess amount (as defined in subparagraph (B)) for the plan for a contract year, subject to the succeeding provisions of this subsection, the organization shall provide to individuals such additional benefits (as the organization may specify) in a value which the Secretary determines is at least equal to the adjusted excess amount (as defined in subparagraph (C)).

children.label: i
children.text: the average of the capitation payments made to the organization under for the plan at the beginning of contract year, exceedssection 1395w–23 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the actuarial value of the required benefits described in under the plan for individuals under this part, as determined based upon an adjusted community rate described in paragraph (3) (as reduced for the actuarial value of the coinsurance, copayments, and deductibles under parts A and B).section 1395w–22(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.heading: Adjusted excess amount
children.label: C Adjusted excess amount
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the “adjusted excess amount”, for an organization for a plan, is the excess amount reduced to reflect any amount withheld and reserved for the organization for the year under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Uniform application
children.label: D Uniform application
children.text: This paragraph shall be applied uniformly for all enrollees for a plan.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), as part of providing any additional benefits required under subparagraph (A), a Medicare+Choice organization may elect a reduction in its payments under with respect to a Medicare+Choice plan and the Secretary shall apply such reduction to reduce the premium under of each enrollee in such plan as provided in .section 1395w–23(a)(1)(A) of this titlesection 1395r of this titlesection 1395s(i) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: may not exceed 125 percent of the premium described under ; andsection 1395r(a)(3) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: shall apply uniformly to each enrollee of the Medicare+Choice plan to which such reduction applies.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: F Construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as preventing a Medicare+Choice organization from providing supplemental benefits (described in ) that are in addition to the health care benefits otherwise required to be provided under this paragraph and from imposing a premium for such supplemental benefits.section 1395w–22(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Stabilization fund
children.label: 2 Stabilization fund
children.text: A Medicare+Choice organization may provide that a part of the value of an excess amount described in paragraph (1) be withheld and reserved in the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund and in the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund (in such proportions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate) by the Secretary for subsequent annual contract periods, to the extent required to stabilize and prevent undue fluctuations in the additional benefits offered in those subsequent periods by the organization in accordance with such paragraph. Any of such value of the amount reserved which is not provided as additional benefits described in paragraph (1)(A) to individuals electing the Medicare+Choice plan of the organization in accordance with such paragraph prior to the end of such periods, shall revert for the use of such trust funds.

children.label: A
children.text: the rate of payment for that service or services which the Secretary annually determines would apply to an individual electing a Medicare+Choice plan under this part if the rate of payment were determined under a “community rating system” (as defined in , other than subparagraph (C)), orsection 300e–1(8) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: such portion of the weighted aggregate premium, which the Secretary annually estimates would apply to such an individual, as the Secretary annually estimates is attributable to that service or services,

children.heading: Determination based on insufficient data
children.label: 4 Determination based on insufficient data
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, if the Secretary finds that there is insufficient enrollment experience to determine an average of the capitation payments to be made under this part at the beginning of a contract period or to determine (in the case of a newly operated provider-sponsored organization or other new organization) the adjusted community rate for the organization, the Secretary may determine such an average based on the enrollment experience of other contracts entered into under this part and may determine such a rate using data in the general commercial marketplace.

children.heading: Prohibition of State imposition of premium taxes
children.label: g Prohibition of State imposition of premium taxes
children.text: No State may impose a premium tax or similar tax with respect to payments to Medicare+Choice organizations under or premiums paid to such organizations under this part.section 1395w–23 of this title

children.heading: Permitting use of segments of service areas
children.label: h Permitting use of segments of service areas
children.text: The Secretary shall permit a Medicare+Choice organization to elect to apply the provisions of this section uniformly to separate segments of a service area (rather than uniformly to an entire service area) as long as such segments are composed of one or more Medicare+Choice payment areas.
